quotes.quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.
quotes.rationale: Identifies an LLM evaluator that ranks drug candidates using supporting literature.

quotes.quote: When comparing the LLM judge’s preferences with experts’, the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10 (Supplementary Figure S11A). This concordance is more than double that expected from random selection. Furthermore, the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts (Supplementary Figure S11B). When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.
quotes.rationale: Reports agreement and consistency evidence for the LLM judge's preferences against experts.

quotes.quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.
quotes.rationale: Shows Robin autonomously analyzes experimental data and routes interpretation into a new candidate round.

quotes.quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.
quotes.rationale: Identifies researchers as the human executors producing and transferring experimental data to Robin.

quotes.quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
quotes.rationale: Separates the Finch computational/statistical analysis channel from the human preprocessing step.

quotes.quote: Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.
quotes.rationale: Identifies a human scientist analysis channel for flow cytometry data.

diagnostics.consequence: Claims about laboratory-ready automation should be limited because human translation remains required for executable protocols.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: For example, while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols—future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution.

diagnostics.statement: Robin does not yet produce precise, executable laboratory protocols.

diagnostics.consequence: The iterative Robin analysis loop lacks packet-supported channel-specific reliability evidence, so its autonomous candidate-generation feedback should not be treated as validated for error, bias, or calibration.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.

diagnostics.statement: No reliability study is reported for Robin's autonomous analysis of experimental data into a new candidate round.

diagnostics.consequence: Human preprocessing and flow cytometry analysis remain part of the validation stack, limiting any coding of full end-to-end autonomous execution.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.

diagnostics.statement: Some experimental data processing and analysis are performed by human scientists rather than Robin.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: For example, while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols—future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution.

recommendations.proposed_action: Evaluate whether future Robin outputs produce precise, executable protocols requiring minimal human translation.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Protocol outputs are executable by laboratory staff with minimal human translation, matching the stated future-iteration aim.
success_check.evidence_needed: A protocol review or execution record for Robin-generated methodologies.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.

recommendations.proposed_action: Report a channel-specific reliability evaluation for Robin's autonomous experimental-data analysis and candidate-generation loop.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Reliability evidence is reported for the same Robin analysis-to-new-candidates channel rather than transferred from the LLM tournament judge.
success_check.evidence_needed: A study comparing Robin's analysis outputs or candidate revisions with an appropriate reference or expert assessment.
success_check.observable: Reliability finding for Robin's autonomous analysis loop.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.

recommendations.proposed_action: Explicitly report which data-analysis stages are human-performed versus system-performed in future evaluations.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Each reported assay analysis stage is assigned to human, Finch, Robin, or another named actor.
success_check.evidence_needed: A stage-by-stage methods description for read processing, differential expression, and flow cytometry analysis.
success_check.observable: Completeness of actor attribution across data-analysis stages.

policy_type: iterative experimental data analysis for candidate generation
